summary: The three types of muscle are skeletal, cardiac, and smooth.
The three types of muscle are voluntary, involuntary, and mixed.
The three types of muscle are striated, non-striated, and elastic.
Skeletal muscle has striations and multiple peripheral nuclei.
Skeletal muscle has striations and a single central nucleus.
Skeletal muscle lacks striations but has peripheral nuclei.
An aponeurosis is a broad flat tendon, while a tendon is a round cord-like attachment.
An aponeurosis is a round cord-like attachment found in the hand.
A tendon is a broad flat sheet of connective tissue on the abdominal wall.
A spurt muscle inserts far from the joint and acts as a prime mover; a shunt muscle inserts close and stabilises.
A spurt muscle inserts close to the joint and stabilises it.
Shunt muscles are always larger than spurt muscles.
Cardiac muscle cells are connected by intercalated discs for coordinated contraction.
Cardiac muscle cells are connected by gap junctions without intercalated discs.
Cardiac muscle cells are multinucleated like skeletal muscle.
Smooth muscle has a single central nucleus and lacks visible striations.
Smooth muscle has multiple peripheral nuclei and faint striations.
Smooth muscle is under voluntary control via somatic motor neurons.
The sarcomere is the functional unit of skeletal muscle, bounded by Z lines.
The sarcomere is bounded by M lines and contains only actin filaments.
The motor unit is the structural unit visible between Z lines.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review muscle features and try again.
